steps.title: Identify the relationship the transition must convey
steps.content: Rogerson's calculation was more accurate than the 1920 one; the next sentence explains why by noting the earlier method's rudimentary equipment. The transition word must signal this explanatory relationship.

steps.title: Apply the correct transition
steps.content:
”Of course,” (B) signals that the explanation is obvious and expected—naturally, a method using only cardboard and string would produce a less precise result, explaining the accuracy difference. The other choices introduce different relationships: “Ultimately” suggests a final conclusion, “Likewise” implies similarity, and “To that end” signals purpose.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
